examples.title: Contract Type Selection Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to recommend appropriate contract type based on project characteristics
examples.code:
FUNCTION SelectContractType(requirementsStability, projectSize, budgetFlexibility)
 BEGIN
 IF requirementsStability == "stable" AND projectSize == "small" THEN
 RETURN "Fixed Price"
 ELSE IF requirementsStability == "unstable" AND budgetFlexibility == "high" THEN
 RETURN "Time and Materials"
 ELSE IF requirementsStability == "unstable" AND budgetFlexibility == "low" THEN
 RETURN "Time and Materials with Cap"
 ELSE IF projectSize == "large" AND requirementsStability == "moderate" THEN
 RETURN "Cost Plus Incentive Fee"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Hybrid (Phased Fixed Price)"
 END IF
 END
examples.explanation: This algorithm helps select contract type based on key factors: requirement stability, project size, and budget flexibility. For stable, small projects Fixed Price is ideal; for unstable requirements with flexible budget, T&M works best.

examples.title: SLA Compliance Checker
examples.description: Function to check if vendor meets SLA requirements and calculate penalties
examples.code:
FUNCTION CheckSLACompliance(actualUptime, actualResponseTime, SLAUptime, SLAResponseTime)
 BEGIN
 uptimePenalty = 0
 responsePenalty = 0
 
 IF actualUptime < SLAUptime THEN
 downtime = SLAUptime - actualUptime
 uptimePenalty = downtime * 1000 // Rs 1000 per 0.1% downtime
 END IF
 
 IF actualResponseTime > SLAResponseTime THEN
 exceededTime = actualResponseTime - SLAResponseTime
 responsePenalty = exceededTime * 500 // Rs 500 per hour exceeded
 END IF
 
 totalPenalty = uptimePenalty + responsePenalty
 
 IF totalPenalty > 0 THEN
 PRINT "SLA Breach: Penalty = Rs" + totalPenalty
 RETURN totalPenalty
 ELSE
 PRINT "SLA Compliant - No penalties"
 RETURN 0
 END IF
 END
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates how SLA compliance is monitored and penalties calculated. It checks uptime and response time metrics against agreed SLA values and computes financial penalties for breaches.

examples.title: Change Request Processing
examples.description: Process to handle contract modifications systematically
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ProcessChangeRequest(changeRequest)
 BEGIN
 // Step 1: Validate Change Request
 IF changeRequest.description == NULL THEN
 RETURN "Invalid: No description provided"
 END IF
 
 // Step 2: Impact Analysis
 costImpact = EstimateCost(changeRequest)
 timeImpact = EstimateTime(changeRequest)
 scopeImpact = AnalyzeScope(changeRequest)
 
 // Step 3: Approval Decision
 IF costImpact > threshold AND timeImpact > timelineLimit THEN
 RETURN "Requires Executive Approval"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Standard Approval"
 END IF
 
 // Step 4: Generate Amendment
 amendment = CreateAmendment(changeRequest, costImpact, timeImpact)
 
 // Step 5: Update Contract
 IF BothPartiesSign(amendment) THEN
 UpdateContract(amendment)
 RETURN "Change Approved and Implemented"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Change Rejected"
 END IF
 END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the systematic process for handling change requests in contract management, ensuring all modifications are properly evaluated, approved, and documented as contract amendments.
